subject: General Intelligence & Reasoning
concept: A series is a sequence of numbers/alphabetical letters or both which follow a particular rule. Each element of series is called a 'term'. We analyse the pattern and find the missing term, next term, or wrong term to continue the pattern.

types.name: Number Series
types.desc: A series made by only numbers or digits.

types.name: Alphabet Series
types.desc: A series made by only alphabetical letters. Use letter positions A=1 to Z=26.

subtypes.name: Alternate
subtypes.desc: A, Z, B, Y → opposite pairs

types.name: Alpha-Numeric Series
types.desc: Combination of letters and numbers. Pattern exists in both separately.

types.name: Mixed Series
types.desc: Combination of two or more different patterns. Check odd and even positions separately.
types.example: Z, L, X, J, V, H, T, F, ?
types.solution: Two series: (i) Z,X,V,T,_ → -2 each → Answer: R | (ii) L,J,H,F,_ → -2 each → Answer: D

types.name: Correspondence Series
types.desc: Three sequences with three different elements (capital letters, numbers, small letters). Each element corresponds to another.

keyPoints: Difference Pattern: Check difference between consecutive terms. Ex: 4,6,9,13,18 → +2,+3,+4,+5
Multiplication Pattern: Each term multiplied. Ex: 3,6,18,72 → ×2,×3,×4
Alternate Pattern: Two sequences simultaneously. Ex: 2,5,3,7,4,9 → Odd:2,3,4 / Even:5,7,9
Combination Pattern: Mix of operations. Ex: 2,6,7,21,22,66 → ×3,+1
Wrong Term: One term breaks the pattern → identify and correct it
strategy: Look for alternate pattern — check odd and even positions separately
For letters — convert to numbers using A=1 to Z=26
importantFacts: Special Series for SSC: Prime (2,3,5,7,11,13), Fibonacci (1,1,2,3,5,8,13), Factorial (1,2,6,24,120)
Opposite letter pairs always sum to 27: A+Z=27, B+Y=27, C+X=27
Big numbers → try division; Small numbers → try difference; Letters → convert to positions
Many SSC questions use multi-layer patterns — always separate odd and even positions first
Common mistakes: ignoring alternate patterns, missing double operations, not converting letters
dates: A=1, M=13, N=14, Z=26 :: Standard letter positions
Question Types :: Complete Series / Find Missing Term / Find Wrong Term
quickRevision:
Step 1: Difference → Step 2: Ratio → Step 3: Alternate positions → Step 4: Square/Cube → Step 5: Combination
Big numbers=division, Small numbers=difference, Letters=convert to numbers
Special series: Primes, Fibonacci, Factorials
